title: Primer design for retrieval of stored polynucleotides
abstract: This disclosure describes techniques to improve the accuracy of random access of data stored in polynucleotide sequence data storage systems. Primers used in polynucleotide sequence replication and amplification can be scored against a number of criteria that indicate the fitness of sequences of nucleotides to function as primers. Primers having scores that indicate a particular fitness to function as primers can be added to a specific group of primers. The primers from the group of primers can be used in amplification and replication of polynucleotide sequences that encode digital data. Additionally, an amount of overlap between primer targets and payloads encoding digital data can be determined. Minimizing the amount of overlap between primer targets and payloads can improve the efficiency of polynucleotide replication and amplification. The bits of the digital data can be randomized to minimize the amount of overlap between payloads encoding the digital data and primer targets.
claims:
Claims 1. A method comprising: generating data indicating a primer sequence; determining a score for the primer sequence based at least partly on an estimated melting temperature of the potential primer sequence and one or more additional criteria; and adding the primer sequence to a group of primers based at least partly on the score being less than a threshold score. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein determining the score for the primer sequence includes: determining a first score for a nucleotide of the primer sequence based at least partly on the estimated melting temperature of the primer sequence; and determining a second score for the nucleotide based at least partly on one of the one or more additional criteria. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein determining the score for the primer sequence based at least partly on the estimated melting temperature of the primer sequence includes determining that the melting temperature is included in a range of melting temperatures. 4. The method of any one of claims 1-3, further comprising: modifying, based at least partly on the score being less than the threshold score, at least one nucleotide of the primer sequence to produce a modified primer sequence; determining an additional score for the modified primer sequence based at least partly on an additional estimated melting temperature of the modified primer sequence and the one or more additional criteria; adding the modified primer sequence to the group of primers at least partly based on the additional score being below the threshold score; and generating a polynucleotide sequence including at least one primer target complementary to at least one of the primer sequence or the modified primer sequence, the polynucleotide sequence also including a payload having another sequence of nucleotides that encodes digital data. 5. A system comprising: a processing unit; a memory in communication with the processing unit, the memory storing computer- readable instructions that when executed by the processing unit perform operations comprising: 

 generating data including a sequence of nucleotides for a primer; determining a respective score for individual nucleotides of the primer for individual criteria of a plurality of criteria, the plurality of criteria being related to a fitness of sequences of nucleotides to be used in polynucleotide sequence amplification and replication; aggregating the respective scores for the individual nucleotides of the primer with respect to the individual criteria of the plurality of criteria to produce an aggregated score; determining that the aggregated score is less than a threshold score; and generating a polynucleotide sequence that includes a primer target at least partially complementary to the primer, an additional primer target at least partially complementary to an additional primer having an additional aggregated score less than the threshold score, and a payload including a nucleotide sequence that encodes digital data. 6. The system of claim 5, wherein the plurality of criteria includes secondary structure formation; and operations further comprise: determining a score for a nucleotide of the primer based at least partly on a probability of the nucleotide participating in the formation of a secondary structure. 7. The system of claim 5 or 6, wherein the plurality of criteria includes primer-dimer formation, and the operations further comprise: determining a score for a nucleotide of the primer based at least partly on a probability of the primer forming a dimer with another primer. 8. The system of claim 7, wherein the plurality of criteria includes length of A/T regions of the sequence of nucleotides, and the operations further comprise: determining that a nucleotide included in the primer is an A or a T; determining that the nucleotide is included in a region of the primer that consists of one or more A's and one or more T's; determining a length of the region; and determining a score for a nucleotide based at least partly on the length of the region. 9. The system of any one of claims 5-8, wherein the plurality of criteria includes length of G/C regions of the sequence of nucleotides, and the operations further comprise: determining that a nucleotide included in the primer is a G or a C; determining that the nucleotide is included in a region of the primer that consists of one or more G's and one or more C's; 

 determining a length of the region; and determining a score for a nucleotide based at least partly on the length of the region. 10. The system of any one of claims 5-9, wherein the plurality of criteria includes G/C content, and the operations further comprise: determining a number nucleotides included in the primer that are G and C in relation to the total number of nucleotides included in the primer; and determining a score for the nucleotide based at least partly on the nucleotide being a G or a C and the number of nucleotides in the primer that are G or C in relation to the total number of nucleotides of the primer. 11. The system of any one of claims 5-10, wherein the plurality of criteria includes nucleotide differentiation, and the operations further comprise: comparing a nucleotide of the primer with one or more additional nucleotides included in the primer; and determining a score for the nucleotide based at least partly on differences between the nucleotide and the one or more additional nucleotides. 12. The system of any one of claims 5-11, wherein the plurality of criteria includes primer similarity, and the operations further comprise: comparing nucleotides of the primer with nucleotides of an additional primer; determining a number of nucleotides of the primer that are the same as the nucleotides of the additional primer and that are located in the same positions; and determining a score for the nucleotide based at least partly on the number of nucleotides of the primer that are the same as the nucleotides of the additional primer and that are located in the same positions. 13. A method comprising: determining one or more characteristics of a first primer sequence; determining a score for the first primer sequence based at least partly on the one or more characteristics in relation to one or more criteria, wherein the one or more criteria indicate a fitness of the first primer sequence to be utilized in polynucleotide sequence amplification; determining that the score is less than a threshold score; adding the first primer sequence to a group of primers; and generating a polynucleotide sequence including a first primer target corresponding to the first primer sequence, a second primer target corresponding to a second primer sequence from the group of primers, and a payload including an additional sequence of 

 nucleotides that encodes digital data. 14. The method of claim 13, further comprising: receiving, from a computing device, a request for the digital data; parsing a data structure to determine that the first primer sequence and the second primer sequence are associated with the digital data; amplifying one or more polynucleotides included in a container of a polynucleotide storage system using the one or more enzymes, the first primer sequence, and the second primer sequence; sequencing the one or more polynucleotide sequences; decoding the one or more polynucleotide sequences to identify a series of bits corresponding to the digital data; and providing the series of bits to the computing device. 15. The method of claim 13 or 14, wherein a first group of polynucleotides stored in a first container of the polynucleotide storage system have a first range of melting temperatures and a second group of polynucleotides stored in a second container of the polynucleotide storage system have a second range of melting temperatures.
